description: §20.8.1 — `<outlet>` honors the universal `if=` structural directive. With `if=@show` and `@show=false`, the outlet region is NOT mounted, so the live DOM contains ZERO `[data-scrml-outlet]` regions. (Regression: the Wave-1a outlet emit branch used to `return` before the generic `if=` guard, so `if=` was silently ignored and the region always rendered.)
rationale: §17.1 `if=` is a universal display/mount directive; the outlet region is a plain DOM region and must gate on `if=` like any element — a false guard means the region is absent from the live tree.
